text: The surgeon incises the peritoneum at the base of the sigmoid mesentery, identifying the inferior mesenteric artery (IMA) circumferentially. Dissection proceeds from medial to lateral, separating the mesocolon from the retroperitoneum. The IMA and its terminal branches are lifted, revealing the left colic artery in a T-shaped configuration.

The surgeon dissects proximally along the left colic artery, revealing the inferior mesenteric vein (IMV). The left colic artery is separated from the IMV, which is then sealed using an energy device, exposing the left colic vein.

The surgeon mobilizes the left colon laterally and inserts the end of a stapler to divide the sigmoid colon distal to the sigmoid junction. The image shows the complete dissection of the area with the left colic artery preserved.
The surgeon completes the procedure by performing a Knight-Griffen anastomosis, visually connecting the bowel ends to restore continuity after resection.
